ABOUT.WORDMARK_ARIA: GoRoute — top of content on this page

HERO.LABEL: Company
HERO.TITLE: About GoRoute
HERO.SUBTITLE: Where your professional roadmap meets real opportunities
HERO.LEDE: GoRoute connects career trajectory planning with direct market access — full-time or freelance — through transparent, skills-based matching.
HERO.CTA_HOME: See the invite on the home page

ORIGIN.LABEL: 01 · origin
ORIGIN.TITLE: Our origin: born from a real market pain
ORIGIN.Q: Have you ever felt your career is scattered across too many places?
ORIGIN.P1: LinkedIn for the résumé, the portfolio for skills, WhatsApp for contacts, openings across dozens of sites… and nowhere that ties career trajectory planning to direct market connection — whether for a steady job or freelance work.
ORIGIN.P2: Companies suffer too: hours filtering résumés that don’t reflect what people can actually do. Great talent is missed because only shiny titles are visible, not real skills.
ORIGIN.P4: No more “just another jobs portal.” No more lonely goal trackers. GoRoute connects your professional evolution map to the right companies — intelligently, transparently, and at scale.

WHAT.LABEL: 02 · product
WHAT.TITLE: What is GoRoute?
WHAT.P1: GoRoute is an ecosystem where professionals build a career roadmap (milestones, skills, objectives) and companies discover that talent through matching grounded in real competencies.
WHAT.QUOTE: A Waze for your professional life — with real opportunities appearing along the route.
WHAT.P_PRO_INTRO: If you’re a professional, GoRoute helps you:
WHAT.PRO_2: See progress on a clear timeline.
WHAT.PRO_3: Get discovered by companies looking for what you actually do — not just your job title.
WHAT.P_CO_INTRO: If you’re a company, GoRoute lets you:
WHAT.CO_1: Post full-time or freelance roles manually (simple form).
WHAT.CO_2: Integrate HR systems via dedicated endpoints for automated job creation.
WHAT.CO_3: Tap a talent base organized by real skills — not keyword stuffing.

AUDIENCE.LABEL: 03 · who it’s for
AUDIENCE.TITLE: Who is GoRoute for?
AUDIENCE.PRO_1: People starting out who want a clear roadmap.
AUDIENCE.PRO_2: People switching fields who need to show momentum.
AUDIENCE.PRO_3: Freelancers who want to be found for focused projects.
AUDIENCE.PRO_4: Experienced people tired of blind résumé roulette.
AUDIENCE.CO_1: Startups that need to hire fast.
AUDIENCE.CO_3: HR teams reducing noise in screening.
AUDIENCE.CO_4: Businesses that need an API to sync jobs automatically.

FEATURES.LABEL: 04 · value
FEATURES.TITLE: Features that matter
FEATURES.R3_PRO: Public profile with an evolution timeline
FEATURES.R3_CO: REST API endpoints for bulk job creation
FEATURES.R4_PRO: Notifications when roles match your trajectory

CONNECTION.LABEL: 05 · flow
CONNECTION.TITLE: How professionals and companies connect
CONNECTION.S1: The professional builds a roadmap — interests, skills, short/medium-term goals.
CONNECTION.S2: The company posts a role — required skills, contract type, location (or remote).
CONNECTION.S3: GoRoute’s engine crosses signals — surfacing people who have or are building the exact skills requested.
CONNECTION.S4: Matches emerge organically — both sides get notified and can start contact inside the platform.
CONNECTION.NOTE: Differentiator: even without 100% of a role’s skills, the roadmap shows momentum — and companies can bet on potential.

DUAL.LABEL: 06 · scale
DUAL.TITLE: Our edge: dual routes (manual + API)
DUAL.P1: Every company has its moment and stack — so we offer two ways to publish jobs:
DUAL.MANUAL: for smaller teams, one-off hires, or early experiments.
DUAL.API: for organizations with HR systems that want automated, secure pushes straight into GoRoute.
DUAL.P2: Start simple; when you scale, plug GoRoute into the workflow you already run — without friction.

VISION.LABEL: 07 · vision
VISION.TITLE: What we’re building
VISION.DECLARATION: We want to be the reference point for anyone serious about professional growth across Brazil and Latin America.
VISION.V1: Someone learning to code can show weekly progress.
VISION.V2: A seasoned expert can land international freelance work without leaving their roadmap.
VISION.V3: A tech company fills roles in 48 hours with people who already wield the tools they use.

QUOTES.LABEL: 08 · social
QUOTES.TITLE: Snippets for social
QUOTES.Q1: Stop sending résumés into the void. Build your professional roadmap and let the right companies find you. GoRoute — your map, your opportunities.
QUOTES.Q2: Tired of résumés that don’t reflect reality? Post jobs manually or integrate via API. Meet GoRoute.
QUOTES.Q3: GoRoute was born from a real pain: professionals without direction and companies without real visibility into skills. We built the bridge.

CLOSING.TITLE: GoRoute — every skill has a path, and every path leads to opportunity.
CLOSING.P2: Let’s build the future of work — together.

FOOTER.TABS_ARIA: Same sections as the side menu

LOGIN.ASIDE_TITLE: Pick up where you left off.
LOGIN.ASIDE_DEK: Plan your next career move with clarity: concrete steps, market context, and tailored suggestions.
LOGIN.ASIDE_PILL1_D: Less guessing—you see what to do now and what comes next.
LOGIN.ASIDE_PILL2_D: Signals that help you decide calmly—without scattered spreadsheets.
LOGIN.ASIDE_PILL3_D: Strong password today; extra verification when you choose to turn it on.
LOGIN.MFA_HINT: If your account has MFA enabled, after your password you will enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
LOGIN.SIGNIN_DEK: Use email and password below. Google, GitHub, and Apple sign-in will appear here once OAuth is connected.
LOGIN.REGISTER_DEK: Just a few fields to start. You can enable MFA later in preferences.
LOGIN.EMAIL_HINT: Same email for account recovery when available.
LOGIN.MFA_HINT_INPUT: Six digits from your authenticator app.
LOGIN.NAME: Full name
LOGIN.REMEMBER: Keep me signed in on this device
LOGIN.SOCIAL_PENDING: OAuth flow is stubbed: this prototype simulates redirect until the backend is available.
LOGIN.COMPANY_DEK: Use your corporate email and password for the company account registered on GoRoute. MFA works the same as for individual accounts.
LOGIN.COMPANY_EMAIL_HINT: Must be the email linked to the company account or allowed by your admin.
LOGIN.COMPANY_SOCIAL_OR: Or continue with your organization provider (OAuth coming soon)
LOGIN.TERMS: I agree to the terms of service and privacy policy.
LOGIN.SUCCESS_REGISTER: Account created. Sign in to continue.
LOGIN.ERR_LOGIN: Check your email and password (minimum 8 characters) and try again.
LOGIN.ERR_NETWORK: Could not reach the server. Ensure the dev proxy is enabled and auth (3010) plus API (3000) are running.
LOGIN.ERR_RATE_LIMIT: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment and try again.
LOGIN.ERR_REGISTER_EXISTS: An account with this email already exists.
LOGIN.ERR_REGISTER_WEAK: Password must be at least 8 characters and include letters, numbers, and a symbol.
LOGIN.COMPANY_NAME_PH: Trade name shown on the platform
LOGIN.ERR_REGISTER_COMPANY: Enter the company name (at least 2 characters).
LOGIN.MFA_DEK: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
LOGIN.MFA_INVALID: Invalid code. Generate a fresh code in your app and try again.

ONBOARDING.TITLE: First-time setup
ONBOARDING.DEK: Complete your profile for a tailored experience. You can skip and fill in later.
ONBOARDING.HEADLINE: Headline / current role
ONBOARDING.AREAS_HINT: Comma-separated slugs from the catalog (e.g. frontend, backend, data).
ONBOARDING.ERR_INVALID_SKILLS: Some tag or skill is not in the allowed list. Check slugs.
ONBOARDING.ERR_NETWORK: Could not save. Please try again.

DASHBOARD.PERSONAL_DEK: Roadmap, skills, and recommendations coming soon.
DASHBOARD.COMPANY_DEK: Job posts and talent search coming soon.
